meta.title: The Long Investor — Asset Recommendations Dashboard
meta.subtitle: Elliott Wave analysis from TLI emails (last 30 days) · Assets & collections parsed from email subjects ($TICKER - COLLECTION) · Click any ticker for full TLI summary

assets.wave: Wave 1 low (~$91) → recovery, now testing multi-year resistance
assets.summary: $BABA continues its strong recovery from $91 to $122, now testing a historically significant resistance line near $121 that has capped price for about five years. TLI still views this as Wave 1 of the broader move and would look to add on any pullback to the 0.618 Fib / 200 WMA around $105–$109 ahead of a breakout attempt above $121, viewing the stock as still severely undervalued similar to prior setups in $OSCR and $UNH.

ZETA jumped 5% then extended gains after-hours following CEO comments repositioning the company as an AI infrastructure business, alongside a newly announced Palantir partnership. TLI notes 12 of 14 covering analysts rate the stock a buy, and views the pop as a fresh higher high building on support at the 50-week MA. A break above the $26 range line would confirm the impulse move toward the Wave 3 target of $37.

assets.wave: Subwave 2 (bullish wedge) — bounced off 0.618 Fib; testing wedge top ahead of 0.5 Fib / 50 Day MA confirmation
assets.entry: $26 (BUY ALERT, added on 0.618 Fib test); wedge top now testing $28.28
assets.summary: TLI added to $WYFI again after Monday's +13% bounce cooled off with a test of the 0.618 Fib, which held and finished the session green. Price has since formed a bullish wedge in what TLI is calling Subwave 2, and the premarket is now testing the wedge's top boundary at $28.28; a breakout would set up a run to the 0.5 Fib at $28.81 and the 50-day MA at $30.78, both of which need to flip to support to confirm the pullback is fully complete.

assets.wave: ABC pullback complete (bounced $11.85 → $14.90); now testing 50 Day MA for confirmation
assets.entry: $14.90 (50 Day MA) — next buy entry on confirmed flip to support
assets.summary: $ADUR's dip to the 200-day MA, 0.618 Fib, and wedge breakout line last week was, in TLI's view, simply a routine ABC pullback with no fundamental catalyst behind it — exactly the kind of three-wave retest expected in a healthy bullish trend. Price has since bounced sharply from $11.85 to $14.90 in just three sessions and is now testing the 50-day MA. TLI says flipping that level to support would put the bulls in full control, with the trend then expected to resume its march toward the prior ATH at $18, marking the next buy entry.

levels.label: Status
levels.value: Bulls in control if 50 Day MA flips to support

assets.wave: Wave 2 (0.786 Fib, ABC) — holding after 39% one-week pullback; watching for higher low before Primary Wave 3
assets.entry: $11.15 (0.786 Fib, confluence support) — holding after brutal pullback; watch for retest/higher low
assets.summary: In a chart-building video, TLI walked through $GRRR's Elliott Wave count after last week's brutal 39% one-week pullback, noting the 0.786 Fib confluence level has held and produced a strong bounce. TLI still isn't certain whether price forms a higher low here or dips to a marginal lower low to shake out weak hands before the next leg, but views either outcome as healthy and reiterates high confidence in the broader bullish structure. The Primary Wave 3 target remains near $34, with the longer-term Cycle Wave 5 target set around $120.

assets.wave: Wave 3 (wedge breakout) — 3-wave corrective pullback, added again on sale
assets.entry: $55–$65 (0.618 Fib pullback in wedge); added at $57.50
assets.summary: TLI added to $ASTS again at $57.50 as the stock continues its 3-wave pullback to the 0.618 Fib within a bullish descending wedge. With no need to trim given the corrective structure of the pullback (versus the broader market's ATHs), TLI is watching for a move back up to the wedge breakout level and the 0.5 Fib at $65.70, while reiterating the long-term 10–20x potential if execution continues on schedule.

SE has broken out of its prior consolidation range with a higher high and higher low, and recent market weakness could pull it back to test support at $98 (0.618 Fib) or $89 (200 WMA). TLI views a retest of either level as an opportunity to add, and confirmation of support there would validate a new Wave 3 impulse toward the long-term target.

TLI sees PFE's pullback to the $23 0.618 Fib as a healthy Wave 2 correction, with price now testing the 50-week moving average at $25.69 from below. The near-term key is flipping that level into support, which would open the door to a new high above the $28 Wave 1 top and eventually the 200 WMA at $30. Long-term Wave 3 target remains $36, and the roughly 6.8% dividend plus an expected rotation into defensive names makes PFE attractive here.

assets.wave: Wave 4 (ABC) — pulled back to strongest Fib support
assets.summary: Silver has pulled back to the 0.618 Fib at $55 — TLI's strongest support level — and the chart shows nothing abnormal. TLI expects smart money to begin rotating back into silver from here and recommends building or adding to positions around current levels.

GOOG closed the week below both the 2.618 Fib confluence at $351 and its rising bearish wedge, which TLI flags as a sign of weakness, though not yet a confirmed breakdown. Confirmation would require a retest and rejection of $351 followed by a new lower low; alternatively a strong earnings season could push price back above $351 to complete Wave 5. TLI notes many members would welcome a drop to the $250 Buy Zone.

AMZN bounced off the 0.618 Fib support at $229 and is now testing resistance at $258, a level TLI calls critical. Reclaiming $258 keeps a Wave 5 rally alive toward $290, while failing to hold it would confirm the bounce was only a Wave B and that a Wave C decline is starting.

AAPL has held up well despite broader market volatility, and HSBC just upgraded the stock from neutral to buy with a $366 price target, closely lining up with TLI's own 3.618 Fib target of $356. TLI isn't recommending any rushed action, but is advising watching for signs of rejection as price nears the Wave 5 target, at which point trimming would be worth considering.

MSFT is trading right around its 200 WMA, and TLI explicitly calls it a buy at this level, framing it as a core, must-own holding for any long-term portfolio. The advice is to dollar-cost-average around the 200 WMA, since historically the market offers this entry roughly every 18-24 months, with further downside simply improving the margin of safety.

SHOP bounced off the 0.618 Fib near $113 and has printed a higher low and higher high, now pressing into the 50 WMA at $136 from below. TLI wants price to reclaim the 50 WMA and turn it into support before getting more constructive; a confirmed break above the prior ATH near $166 would remove doubt that Wave 5 is underway, with $227 as the eventual target.

PLTR is holding above the $125 support line after its Wave C flush to $106 (where Burry closed half his short), but price remains inside a descending triangle while tech stays under pressure. TLI sees current levels as fine for accumulation, but says value-focused buyers should wait for a deeper pullback to $85–$106, where the rising 200 WMA adds confluence support.

TLI sees BIDU, alongside BABA, as undervalued because of a hidden AI stake in a Chinese AI chip company worth nearly as much as BIDU's entire market cap. The current pullback below the 200 WMA, right after a head-and-shoulders pattern completed, is framed as an opportunity rather than a warning sign, drawing a parallel to last September's rally from $90 to $170. TLI recommends having hedges ready in case broader market weakness returns.

levels.label: Resistance
levels.value: $90 (prior breakout, analog to Sept rally to $170)

assets.summary: TLI continues to recommend buying NVO, having called it a buy in the mid-$30s, mid-$50s, and again now near $50, rewarding patient accumulation despite market skepticism. He believes NVO has entered early-stage Wave 3 after breaking above the 50 WMA and printing a higher high, and stresses it's still early to add under $60. The roadmap calls for a Wave 4 pullback to $53 before Wave 5 targets the 200 WMA at $82 and eventually new all-time highs near $150.

assets.wave: Wave C completing — watching for $168 (0.78 Fib) to flip to support
assets.summary: $CRM has bounced out of its Buy Zone and TLI is now watching for the price to flip the 0.78 Fib at $168 into support. A breakout above $180 would target the 50-week MA at $213, and clearing the 200-week MA at $235 would be the clear signal that $CRM has entered Bull Case territory. No changes to the broader thesis — holding $168 remains the key.

assets.wave: Primary Wave 2 (ABC complete) — 3-wave pullback to 0.618 Fib support; awaiting 200 WMA flip
assets.summary: NFLX has pulled back in a clean three-wave ABC correction that's now testing the 0.618 Fib near $61, a level TLI calls a much stronger, healthier setup than before. TLI wants to see the 200-week moving average at $70 flip into support as the next low-risk entry signal, and from there is watching for price to challenge the 50 WMA at $97 from below. The tone has turned constructive again after the post-earnings breakdown, though confirmation is still pending.

assets.wave: Wave 2 (complete) → Wave 3 pending — testing 50 WMA resistance for third week
assets.entry: Bounced out of $608–$650 buy zone; now testing 50 WMA at $685 (third week)
assets.summary: REGN has bounced out of its $608–$650 buy zone and is now testing the 50-week moving average at $685 for a third consecutive week. TLI expects this level to break and flip to support, opening a run toward the 200 WMA at $776 — a level that would confirm the stock has moved into Bull Case territory with Wave 3 targeting $1166. TLI continues to view REGN as a fundamentally strong company worth adding to for members who haven't already.

TSLA has broken below its 50 WMA, shifting focus to wedge support at $370; as long as that holds, TLI isn't concerned. He's explicitly waiting for a breakout above $487 before considering the stock to have entered the Bull Case, with the Wave 3 target unchanged at $754.

NIO is testing the confluence of the wedge's lower support and the 0.618 Fib at $4.95. A failure to hold there opens a deeper slide to the 0.78 Fib near $4.11, which would complete Wave 2. TLI's near-term focus is simply holding within the wedge and then attempting to challenge the 50 WMA at $5.69 from below, with the Wave 3 target unchanged at the 1.618 Fib of $12.

NKE remains squeezed inside its falling wedge after briefly dipping below the 0.78 Fib near $45. Friday's 3.7% bounce was an encouraging first step, but TLI wants to see the 0.78 Fib reclaimed to validate the reversal. Failure to reclaim that level keeps a deeper pullback toward the 0.887 Fib around $28 in play, so support is not yet confirmed.

assets.target: $44 initial Wave 3 target, extending toward 2.0-2.618 Fib for Wave 5 / Cycle Wave 3
assets.summary: OSCR is moving in tandem with UNH, and TLI plans to add to both if OSCR pulls back while UNH works through its own Wave 2, calling it a two-for-one buying opportunity. OSCR's rally off its bottom has been more aggressive with more attractive upside targets than UNH, and it doesn't strictly need to fall further since it already produced a shallow Wave 2 dip.

BTC has spent the year consolidating right at its 200-week moving average and the 0.618 Fib, a zone TLI calls long-term fair value. Despite severe selling pressure across tech, BTC has held this level, which he interprets as smart money quietly accumulating while retail capitulates; he states BTC and ETH are buys here.

levels.label: Note
levels.value: BTC and ETH both flagged as adds

assets.summary: PEP bounced off blue support at $127 up to the 200 WMA near $170 but rejected heavily. TLI tags it 'Safe Haven soon' — a defensive name to watch as rotation into safe havens builds, but not an entry yet.

TLI expects members to rotate into Safe Havens over the coming months and keeps Palladium on watch, but the structure is still corrective. TLI advises waiting for support to confirm before treating it as an entry.

AMD broke down out of a rising wedge and below its 50-day moving average, which TLI calls the expected Wave 4 pullback rather than a bearish signal. He wants price to dip further to test the 0.382 Fib near $432 for support before Wave 5 can begin, with no change to the long-term target.

Visa spent three weeks battling the $366 resistance (1.618 Fib), pulled back three-wave style to the 0.38 Fib, and has now bounced convincingly, which TLI reads as Wave 5 getting underway. A confirmed break and hold above $366 would open the path to $411. TLI notes this chart has tracked their original wave count from three years ago without needing revision.

UNH rallied into the 200 WMA post-earnings as expected, got rejected, and lost the $437 support level, closing the week at $427. TLI wants the pullback to finish forming a clean three-wave correction and land in the $359–$383 Buy Zone, noting the 50 WMA and 0.618 Fib converge near $334 as a deeper downside possibility. TLI would add to both UNH and OSCR if either retraces into its buy zone, but no action is being taken yet.

assets.wave: Wave 4 pullback (from Wave 3 high $13.31) — confluence support forming at 50 Day MA / 0.38 Fib
assets.entry: $11.73 (50 Day MA / 0.38 Fib confluence) — next low-risk entry
assets.target: $18.30 (cup top, Wave 5) — watch resistance near $16 (2.618 Fib) first
assets.summary: $DCTH continues to trade a textbook pattern, with Wave 3 extending just above the 1.618 Fib to $13.31 before beginning its Wave 4 pullback. TLI is watching the confluence of the 50-day MA and 0.38 Fib near $11.73 as the next low-risk entry for those looking to add ahead of Wave 5, though some resistance is expected near the 2.618 Fib at $16 before price can reach the initial Wave 5 target at $18.30, the top of the cup formation.

assets.wave: Wave 2 (0.38 Fib tested) — +3% bounce; watching $13 Bull Case line to flip to support
assets.entry: $12 (0.38 Fib, being tested) — defended so far
assets.summary: $ZVRA is testing the 0.38 Fib support at $12, with a +3% bounce yesterday marking an encouraging first step for buyers defending the level. TLI's next focus is flipping the green Bull Case line at $13 into support, noting some resistance there is normal after an already-attempted breakout. TLI reiterates zero changes to the wave count, with Wave 3 still targeting the 1.618 Fib at $22.

MELI remains back above the 200 WMA and is holding up better than the broader market, down only about 1% on a weak day. TLI's next focus is reclaiming the 0.38 Fib at $1,863, with the $2,000 confluence of the 50 WMA, bull-case trendline and psychological resistance acting as a magnet above that. A break above $2,000 would put the previous highs back in view.

assets.wave: Wave B (amber Fib test, 2nd week) — testing 0.5 Fib at $212, watching 0.618 Fib at $218
assets.summary: $NVDA rallied about 3% to retest the 0.5 Fib at $212 for a second consecutive week, and TLI sees room for price to push through to the 0.618 Fib at $218. A break above $218 would likely open a retest of all-time highs near $236, with $NVDA's tendency to run into earnings — still roughly a month away — raising the odds of fresh ATHs or even a melt-up scenario toward the Wave 5 target of $266. TLI is watching for a test of $218 next.

TOST was added to the watchlist in early June after dropping into TLI's Wave 2 buy zone between the 0.618 and 0.786 Fib. That call has played out, with price reclaiming the 200 WMA at $27.49, and attention now shifts to a challenge of the 50 WMA at $32.91. The long-term Wave 3 target remains the 1.618 Fib at $83.

assets.entry: $30 (50 WMA needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: JD is trading above its 50-week moving average near $30, but TLI wants that level to actually flip into support before treating it as a confirmed low-risk entry. The next test after that would be the 200 WMA at $34, which lines up with a wedge breakout level. The long-term Wave 3 target remains unchanged at $68.

assets.wave: Wave 2 (0.618 Fib defended) — up 11% on strong volume; watching for 50 WMA reclaim
assets.summary: $ONDS tested the 0.618 Fib perfectly as support and reacted with an 11% rally on 283 million shares of volume, exactly the kind of momentum TLI wants to see. Premarket strength has continued, but TLI still needs to see buyers reclaim the 50-week MA at $8.86 before ruling out a deeper pullback toward the 200 WMA. For now, the focus stays on holding above the 0.618 Fib and pushing through $8.86 from below.

assets.wave: Wave C of Wave 4 — pulled back to confluence support ahead of Wave 5
assets.entry: $31 (200 Day MA / 0.38 Fib confluence, Wave 4 support)
assets.summary: $HIMS is playing out as a textbook chart — Wave 3 completed just shy of the 1.618 Fib at $40, and price has pulled back for Wave 4 to the confluence of the 200-day MA and 0.38 Fib near $31. TLI wants to see buyers defend this level, which would set up the start of Wave 5 toward the unchanged $54 target, with an FDA meeting also on the near-term horizon.

TLI notes SOFI broke above the $18.80 triangle resistance but failed to hold it through Thursday's market weakness, then rebounded strongly this week toward $19. A sustained hold above $18.80 would confirm Wave 3 is underway, with the 1.618 Fib extension at $23.40 as the long-term target.

assets.entry: Hold above $4.44 (0.618 Fib); reclaim $5.78 (50 WMA) to confirm Bull Case
assets.summary: OPEN has spent most of 2026 chopping sideways between the 0.618 Fib at $4.44 and the 0.5 Fib at $5.67. A break below $4.44 without a quick reclaim risks a fast drop to the 200 WMA near $3.09, so TLI wants that floor defended. Reclaiming the 50 WMA at $5.78 is the trigger he's waiting on to call OPEN's Bull Case official, with the Wave 3 target unchanged at $20.

assets.target: $182-$192 (200 WMA / wedge breakout / 0.618 Fib resistance cluster)
assets.summary: TLI highlights COIN consolidating below its 200-week moving average at an attractive $164, within striking distance of a confluence of resistance levels: the 200 WMA and wedge breakout level both near $182, and the 0.618 Fib just above at $192. Clearing that cluster would flip all three into support and signal a strong reversal. With crypto broadly trending in the right direction, TLI recommends a starter position in COIN here for investors seeking equity exposure to the crypto space.

TLI calls HYPE's setup clean and bullish after price broke above the $59 Bull Case line and retested the confluence with the 50-day moving average twice. The near-term trigger is a breakout above $75; clearing it opens a path to the 1.618 Fib Wave 3 target of $102, provided price holds above the 50-day MA near $63.

assets.wave: Wave 3 (early) — confluence support held after Thursday overshoot
assets.summary: No material change to the $CIFR chart — the confluence support at the 200-day MA and 0.618 Fib near $18.28 held despite a brief overshoot during Thursday's broad selling, and price has bounced strongly since reclaiming the level, alongside sector peers $NBIS, $IREN and $WYFI. TLI is watching for the 50-day MA to flip to support and the $25 Bull Case trigger line, which would open the path to the $52–53 Wave 3 target.

assets.wave: Wave 4 (flush and recovery) — bounced off $165 low; testing 0.38 Fib at $200 for support confirmation
assets.entry: $200 (0.38 Fib) — needs to flip and hold as support
assets.summary: $NBIS flushed down to $165 last Thursday in a broad market capitulation move, but TLI viewed the selloff as a fake-out and added to existing positions, staying firm on the Wave 4 pullback thesis. Price has since bounced from $165 to $216 in three sessions, and TLI still needs to see $200 flip to confirmed support — the standard level for a Wave 4 — before buyers step back in aggressively. A hold above $200 keeps Wave 5 on track for the $411 target.

assets.wave: Complex WXY correction (~257 days sideways); reclaimed 0.5 Fib & horizontal resistance, awaiting support confirmation
assets.summary: TLI's video update on $IREN notes the stock has been range-bound in a complex WXY correction for roughly 257 days, but price has now reclaimed the 0.5 Fib and a key horizontal resistance level following new contract news. TLI estimates fair value around $92 (about 120% upside from current levels) and is watching for a support confirmation before calling the correction complete, while flagging renewed geopolitical risk from Iran as a near-term caution.

assets.wave: ABC correction (Wave A completing) — Wave B relief bounce underway
assets.entry: $798 (0.38 Fib, Wave B support); next Buy Zone at $657 (0.5 Fib)
assets.summary: $MU is seeing buyers defend the 0.38 Fib at $798, but TLI cautions against chasing here, viewing this as a typical Wave B bounce within an ongoing correction. A break above the 50-day MA at $938 would bring resistance into play between $1,016–$1,073 — a second opportunity for existing holders to trim. TLI's next Buy Zone would open up around $657 (0.5 Fib) should the pullback extend.

assets.entry: No confirmed zone yet — await support / channel breakout
assets.summary: TLI notes the space sector, including SPCX, remains in a downward trend since its post-IPO pump and is still hunting for a support level. Price action is confined to a tight descending channel, and the analyst thinks a fresh catalyst will be needed before that channel breaks. No actionable entry has been defined yet, so this stays a watch-and-wait situation.

NU has pulled back in three waves to the 0.78 Fib retracement at $11.18, just above the 200-week moving average, and buyers appear to be defending that zone. TLI is now watching for a reclaim of the 50 WMA at $15 and a flip of the $16.18 Bull Case line to support to confirm the bullish scenario, with the long-term Wave 3 target unchanged at $29.

assets.wave: Higher highs/higher lows since mid-June low, basing above support trendline
assets.entry: Below $2,469 (200 WMA); next hurdle $2,175 (200 Day MA)
assets.summary: $ETH continues to look bullish, up +28% from its mid-June low with a pattern of higher highs and higher lows. The 200-day MA near $2,175 is the next hurdle before a retest of the 200-week MA at $2,469, which TLI is confident will be tested again. TLI remains comfortable accumulating and staking below the 200 WMA, and would consider the pullback complete — opening the door to a run at all-time highs — once $2,469 flips to support.

TLI has tracked this REIT since 2023, citing its low-risk business model (tenants bear operating risk), strong management, and a safe ~7% dividend. After years of stacked higher lows, a liquidity grab has now swept those lows and price sits at strong horizontal support near the 0.382 Fib inside a roughly five-year falling-wedge consolidation, which TLI views as a long-term (3-4 year) accumulation opportunity worth considering.

ALB rallied in a clean five-wave advance and has since corrected in a textbook three-wave (ABC) pullback, briefly dipping into TLI's Buy Zone between the 0.5 Fib ($134) and 0.618 Fib ($114). The near-term focus is reclaiming the 50 WMA at $137, then clearing the 200 WMA at $146, en route to a longer-term higher high above $220.

RDDT's Wave 2 pullback bottomed in textbook fashion at the 0.618 Fib near $131, and price has since strung together higher highs and higher lows, clearing the 50-week MA near $190. The next objective is the Subwave 3 target at the 1.618 Fib around $232, contingent on holding that $190 level.

CELH has broken out of its multi-month descending wedge and is consolidating with strength around the 0.786 Fib near $30, a pattern TLI compares to how SE based out before its next leg higher. TLI expects CELH to drift higher into the roughly August 11 earnings date on its way toward the 200-week moving average near $44. With fundamentals healthy and the stock decoupled from broader market weakness, TLI believes starting a position here is reasonable.

TLI's analyst walks through two Elliott Wave counts for Ferrari, favoring a Wave 4 pullback scenario with a deeper-than-usual 0.618 retracement (justified since Wave 2 was shallow and Wave 3 ran long). Price sits below the 200-week MA with confluence support just below at the 50-week MA and 200-day MA; reclaiming that zone would set up a push toward Wave 5, framed as an interesting buy spot given Ferrari's steady growth and margins.

assets.wave: Impulse off 2021 top, now completing an ABC corrective pullback
assets.summary: TLI's morning scan flagged CTSH as one of the most oversold S&P 500 names on fundamentals, with a clean corrective ABC decline that broke the 200 WMA and is closing in on the 0.618 Fib near $36. TLI wants support to hold above that level before turning more constructive; growth is lacking but a couple of stronger earnings prints could set up a reversal back toward the 200 WMA around $70.

assets.wave: Wave 3 of impulse, following a Wave 2 correction
assets.summary: TLI describes SEZL's Wave 2 as a clean pullback that held the 0.618-0.78 Fib band before breaking out above the 0.618 Fib near $71 and flipping the 50 WMA to support, powering the stock to $171. The next key test is flipping the Wave 1 top near $186 into support, which would confirm continuation toward the 1.618 Fib extension at $350; TLI notes zero changes to this count since first charted.

assets.wave: Pullback testing 0.78 Fib support (no formal count)
assets.summary: TLI notes Richtech Robotics has pulled back to test the 0.78 Fib near $1.80, which is now needed to hold as support, with price recovering to $1.97 in premarket. TLI frames robotics as a likely next hot theme and is comfortable holding, potentially adding if the Fib support holds; a reclaim of the 50-day MA around $2.50 would confirm the pullback is over.

assets.wave: 5-wave Wave 1 impulse complete; Wave 2 pullback to 0.618/0.5 Fib confluence
assets.entry: $3.52–$3.62 (0.618 Fib / 0.5 Fib confluence with 50 Day MA)
assets.summary: $GRAB has moved into TLI's Buy Zone following a clean 5-wave impulse higher off the June channel breakout, now pulling back in a standard Wave 2 correction to the 0.618 Fib and 50-day MA confluence near $3.52–$3.62. TLI remains bullish long-term, calling $GRAB a hedge against US market risk, and is watching for this level to hold as a higher low before the next impulse leg toward the $10 Wave 3 target.

assets.wave: Impulse count intact (pending) — lost 0.618 Fib/200 Day MA at $4.64; watching for a higher low before Wave 3 resumes
assets.entry: Below $4.64 (0.618 Fib / 200 Day MA) — needs a higher low; reclaim of $4.64 next confirmation
assets.summary: $ASTI lost the 0.618 Fib/200-day MA confluence at $4.64 amid broader market weakness, a setback TLI attributes largely to macro pressure rather than a change in the underlying setup. TLI is now watching for price to carve out a higher low during this pullback, which would keep the impulse count alive, with reclaiming $4.64 as the next confirmation step before Wave 3 can resume toward the $16.85 target.

levels.label: Status
levels.value: Watching for higher low to keep impulse intact

assets.wave: No formal wave count given (tweet-style call)
assets.summary: In a tweet-style post, TLI argues EBAY is headed to $180 from its current $110, framing this in the context of GME's rumored takeover interest. TLI dismisses GME's bid as unrealistic, saying GME can't afford anywhere near fair value, implying EBAY is significantly undervalued relative to TLI's target.

TLI says ABCL's Wave 2 shook out weak hands during the October market crash but the impulse count never changed. Price has made a higher high after retesting the channel breakout at $5 and is back to $7.88, confirming Wave 3 is underway, reinforcing the long-standing 1.618 Fib target.

TLI notes SIDU retraced deep to the confluence of the 0.618 Fib and 200-day MA at $2.44, then bounced 4.4% with premarket already up another 3%, showing buyers stepping in. As long as price holds above the March Wave 2 lows the chart remains valid, with the next hurdle reclaiming the 50-day MA at $3.86 as support.

assets.entry: $62 (50 WMA / Bull Case line, needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: TLI notes MP has consolidated sideways around its blue Bull Case trendline for 8 months, a level now aligning closely with the 50-week MA at $62. Flipping that level to support would confirm entry into the Bull Case, with the next target being prior highs near $100 en route to the longer-term Wave 3 target of $190.

assets.wave: Wave 4 (pullback to $100 support / 50 Day MA) — Wave 3 already hit 1.618 Fib at $119; Wave 5 next
assets.entry: $100 (Wave 4 support / 50 Day MA) — buy attractive here
assets.summary: $HOOD has played out almost exactly as TLI called it last year, pulling back in three waves to the 0.618 Fib at $62 before launching a new impulse that saw Wave 3 hit the 1.618 Fib at $119. Price has now pulled back for Wave 4 to support at $100, coinciding with the 50-day MA, and TLI expects that support to hold and Wave 5 to carry price to the 2.618 Fib target of $150 next. TLI calls the current $100 level a very attractive buy, part of a larger structure that could eventually bring $HOOD to a primary target of $300.

assets.entry: Above 2.618 Fib (extended Wave 5, no new entry called)
assets.summary: In a tweet, TLI highlights Goldman Sachs is up over +900% since the 2020 Covid crash low and has pushed above the 2.618 Fib, the level where Wave 5 typically completes. TLI poses whether the rally can extend further to the 3.618 Fib, implying another possible +30% move before a correction sets in.

TLI notes ACN is in a deep Wave 2 correction, coming within touching distance of the 0.78 Fib at $106. The near-term roadmap is a three-step recovery: hold above the 0.78 Fib at $106, reclaim the 0.618 Fib at $173, then target the 50-week MA at $229 from below.

assets.wave: Possible Death Cross correction (no formal count)
assets.summary: NOW remains in a cautious phase — TLI isn't yet convinced a new impulse wave has begun and is watching for the higher low to hold at the 0.618 Fib near $94; a failure there risks a deeper pullback. A Death Cross has now confirmed on the weekly chart, though TLI notes the last such cross in Feb 2023 preceded a strong rally. If the higher low holds, the next objective is the two moving averages converging near $140.

PG bounced off the 0.5/0.618 Fib support at $137 after an earlier rejection at the $165 resistance line, and is again attempting to bounce from that same level. TLI wants a break above the $150-154 WMA cluster and a flip of $165 from resistance to support before calling the Bull Case confirmed; a 2.8% dividend adds support to the thesis.

TLI describes a textbook ABC correction for Wave 2, with price breaking above the confluence of the 50 and 200 week moving averages at €11. The next hurdle is the blue resistance line at €15; a break there confirms a higher high and Wave 3 underway, targeting the 1.618 Fib at €21, with a Golden Cross also close to forming.

assets.entry: No new entry offered — price continuing steady uptrend
assets.summary: TLI notes this chart rarely needs updating since it has stayed consistent, continuing to print higher highs and higher lows. The Wave 3 target remains the 1.618 Fib at $119, with a cup formation projected to complete at the $136 resistance line, and the longer-term Wave 5 target unchanged at $165.

assets.company: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc · NYSE
assets.summary: HASI is battling the blue Bull Case line at $39, a level TLI says will be telling. A rejection here would likely mean a pullback to the 0.618 Fib/200 WMA confluence at $30, which TLI flags as the next low-risk entry; reclaiming $39 followed by a higher high above $44 opens the path to the Wave 3 target of $59.

QCOM jumped after Micron's earnings and TLI calls the pullback level very attractive, having run from $123 to $240 since April before finding support at the confluence of the wedge breakout, blue support line, and 0.5 Fib at $190. TLI is bullish, saying QCOM could be the next runner behind INTC and MU, targeting the Wave 3 level of $417 over 12-24 months.

assets.entry: $89 - $70 (0.5/0.618 Fib Buy Zone, if 200 WMA rejects)
assets.summary: TLI calls KKR a good long-term investment, with the short-term focus on whether price can flip the 200 WMA at $96 to support. A rejection there could bring a deeper pullback into the Buy Zone at the 0.5 Fib ($89) and 0.618 Fib ($70), which TLI would view as an opportunity to open a position, scaling in with multiple buys given it's still early to rule out a deeper pullback.

assets.entry: $111 (blue support line); $78-$89 secondary zone if $111 fails
assets.summary: ON dropped 11% along with other semis and TLI is watching whether buyers defend the $111 support line; if that fails, the $78-$89 zone (0.5-0.618 Fib) becomes the next support. A weekly Golden Cross is about to form (50 WMA crossing above 200 WMA), an added bullish signal ahead of the Wave 3 target.

assets.entry: $59 (confluence resistance, needs to flip to support)
assets.summary: OKLO remains inside its wedge pattern, having just retested the 0.78 Fib at $52.98. TLI wants to see price reclaim the $59 confluence level to open a path toward the 50 Day MA at $65, with a breakout above the $68 wedge resistance setting up a move to the 200 Day MA at $85 and the unchanged long-term Wave 3 target.

TLI notes DUOL has bounced roughly 50% off its lows, from $88 to $125, using it as a case study in mean reversion: buyers who accumulated at $100 or below are already sitting on sizeable gains simply from price reverting toward its 200 WMA, independent of the company's fundamentals. TLI suspects price can continue back toward that 200 WMA even from current levels.

assets.wave: Bullish descending wedge breakout confirmed; testing 0.618 Fib as new support
assets.summary: $DKNG broke out of its bullish descending wedge, with Michael Burry disclosed to have taken a position — TLI had already added at $23 ahead of the move. Price is now testing the 0.618 Fib at $24 as support, with likely sideways consolidation into August 6th earnings. A strong print could send shares toward the Wave 3 target near $39, reverting toward the 200-week MA at $33. TLI calls this level a low-risk buy given the improved margin of safety versus the $19 downside case.

assets.entry: Valuation-based entry per Q2'26 model (see attached PDF)
assets.summary: This dispatch delivers TLI's Q2'26 valuation update on ASML as a standalone PDF attachment rather than inline commentary, so the specific figures and thesis details aren't visible in the email body itself. Given no new bearish or bullish language appears in the message text, the existing Bull Case stance and Hold analyst rating are carried forward unchanged pending review of the full report.
assets.target: 2.0 Fib extension (beyond reached Wave 3 target of €1,617)

assets.summary: PYPL's price action is being underpinned by an active takeover approach from Stripe and Advent at $60/share, which the board has rejected as inadequate but continues to negotiate. TLI believes the takeover interest will keep supporting shares toward the 200 WMA near $66, and expects a deal to eventually be reached given years of weak shareholder returns.

ABNB had another quiet week with no changes to the thesis. TLI's focus remains on the stock holding above its two key weekly moving averages near $130; as long as that support holds, the next target in view is $182, with no action recommended since the setup is unchanged and intact.

MOS is rallying off Wave 2 support at $25 as Middle East supply pressure lifts fertilizer prices, and price has already reclaimed the 200-day MA at $31 within days. TLI still sees the setup as intact, preferring either a 200-day flip to support at $31 or a shallower retest of the 50-day MA at $27 as an add zone. The Wave 3 target sits at the 1.618 Fib near $49.

VSXY smashed the quarter with an EPS beat of roughly +80%, validating TLI's original thesis first shared last July at $18. Wave 2 held cleanly at the 0.5 Fib and 200-day MA around $40, and the share price has since ripped from $40 to $75 in two weeks. TLI leaves the Wave 3 target at $128 and the eventual Wave 5 target at $181 unchanged.

TLI flags a very bullish setup on Cocoa futures: price has held a strong ascending triangle for months and is now testing the breakout level at $4,800. He believes once $4,800 flips to support, price can run cleanly to the 200 WMA at $5,600 before meeting the larger channel resistance for the next breakout test. Flagged as a name worth watching for members trading futures via Plus500.

assets.entry: $108-$113 (50 WMA / 0.5 Fib confluence, next Wave 2 entry)
assets.summary: TGT has rallied strongly and TLI wants to see it test resistance at the 200 WMA near $131 to complete Wave 1 before a pullback into Wave 2. The 50 WMA is curling higher toward $108 and converging with the 0.5 Fib at $113, and TLI flags that confluence as the next low-risk entry once the pullback arrives. The Wave 3 target sits at the 1.618 Fib extension near $187, and the stock also carries a healthy 3.4% dividend.

DASH is showing strength after Wave C held support at the confluence of the 0.5-0.618 Fib and the 200 WMA. Price is now printing higher highs and higher lows, and TLI is watching for a hold above the 50 WMA at $206 to confirm a move toward the next resistance at $257. TLI reads this as Wave 1 of a new Primary Wave 3, with a long-term target of $539.

assets.wave: Wave count pending (full detail in Deep Dive PDF)
assets.entry: Not disclosed in email body (see attached Deep Dive PDF)
assets.summary: This TLI post is a 'Deep Dive' delivered as a standalone PDF attachment; the email itself contains only the newsletter's standard social-link and footer boilerplate with no inline thesis text. Because the detailed Elliott Wave count, support/entry levels, and price targets live only inside that PDF, no new technical specifics could be extracted from this message. The prior Watchlist status and Hold rating are carried forward pending direct review of the report.

levels.label: Note
levels.value: Full analysis not present in email body

assets.entry: 0.786 Fib retracement + declining wedge + 50-day MA confluence
assets.summary: After a textbook 5-wave impulse to all-time highs, LULU corrected in an A-B-C decline that briefly violated the wave-1 high, an unusual structure. Price now sits at the 0.786 Fib retracement in a declining wedge with 50-day MA confluence, and TLI is watching for a capitulation bounce with a higher high and higher low before treating it as a bottom.

GPC has spent almost four years in a long Wave 2 base and TLI is now watching for a strong Wave 3 breakout. The key test is the confluence of the wedge breakout and the 200 WMA at $139 - a flip to support opens a move to $187, and a break above $187 would confirm Wave 3 toward $317. Bulls may open a small starter position now at the lower-risk entry.

assets.company: Vanguard FTSE Canadian High Dividend Yield ETF
assets.summary: After nearly two years of Wave 2 consolidation, VDY broke out into what TLI is treating as Wave 3, now approaching the 1.618 Fib near $79. A rejection there could justify trimming 20-30% of a position, while those looking to add should watch for a Wave 4 pullback toward $64. TLI calls the chart textbook so far.

FCX has a strong confluence level at the top of Wave 1 with the 50 WMA at $53, and a retest here would offer bulls another buying opportunity. The 1 Fib at $71 has acted as resistance multiple times and needs to flip to support to confirm Wave 3 is underway, with a longer-term target at the 1.618 Fib near $101.

AVAV's Wave C decline dropped below the 200 WMA to the 0.78 Fib at $124 before buyers stepped in, and price has spent two weeks challenging the 200 WMA at $167. TLI is watching for that level to flip to support and a break above the 0.618 Fib at $187 to confirm the Bull Case, which would target the 50 WMA at $251 next.

TLI is turning increasingly bullish on metals into September, and unlike silver's parabolic move, copper has stayed in a more controlled, steady Wave 3 uptrend over the last two years. A flip of $6.50 to support would confirm the next leg toward the 1.618 Fib target near $8, and TLI calls it a solid chart for the next 18 months.

assets.wave: Wave A complete (5 waves down) → awaiting Wave B
assets.entry: $534 (0.5 Fib / 200 Day MA / 50 Day MA confluence) - Wave B bounce/trim zone
assets.summary: TLI counts five waves down on INTU, consistent with a completed Wave A. The focus now shifts to a Wave B relief bounce toward $534, a level reinforced by the 0.5 Fib and confluence with both the 200-day and 50-day MAs, where resistance and the start of Wave C are expected. Since Wave B typically unfolds in three waves (ABC), TLI flags this bounce as a second chance for existing holders to trim before the next leg lower.

levels.label: Note
levels.value: Trim opportunity into bounce, Wave C to follow

assets.summary: QQQ broke down out of its bullish wedge and pulled back to roughly $686, just above the 0.38 Fib near $674, pressured by geopolitical tension, cooling memory stocks and Chinese AI competition. TLI argues this is still a standard 3-wave correction rather than a confirmed top, since a genuine top would require a five-wave decline that hasn't materialized, and sees no reason to sell into earnings season.

SPY mirrored QQQ's shallow 3-wave pullback to the 0.236-0.38 Fib zone before bouncing strongly, reclaiming the 1.618 Fib at $738 and finishing the week within reach of all-time highs on rotation into names like AAPL. TLI sees no signs of a top forming and expects a breakout above $760 to confirm a fresh ATH.
assets.company: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust · NYSE Arca

assets.entry: 50 WMA support (repeatedly held, price ~20% below 200 WMA)
assets.summary: UPS is still roughly 20% below its 200 WMA near $135, but has repeatedly held support at the 50 WMA with buyers stepping in convincingly, producing a sequence of higher highs and higher lows consistent with early Wave 3. TLI calls it one of the safer opportunities in the market right now, helped by a ~5.8% dividend yield, with the Wave 3 target set at $159.

assets.wave: Testing 50 WMA resistance, pre-Bull Case breakout
assets.summary: META is challenging the 50 WMA at $658 from below, which TLI says is normal and not a concern. A clean break above the $680 breakout level would put META back into Bull Case territory with the next resistance at $741. If $680 flips to support and $741 is subsequently cleared, TLI sees the Bullish target at the 0.618 Fib near $914 coming into play.

assets.entry: $14.91 (Bull/Bear Case line, needs flip to support)
assets.summary: CLSK jumped +20% premarket after signing a 20-year, $6.6B lease (up to $11.6B with extension options) for a Sandersville, GA data-center campus deploying 175MW of IT load starting Q4 2027. The move brings price right to the $14.91 Bull/Bear Case battleground level that's been fought over since Feb 2024; flipping it to support opens a run to $23, and clearing $23 targets the top blue line at $42.

assets.entry: $57 (200 WMA flipping to support / historical blue-line level)
assets.summary: TLI is flagging RBLX after it dipped below the 200-week moving average and has since been working back up to reclaim $57 as support, a level that also lines up with a historical support/resistance zone. Following a 3-wave (ABC) pullback from the recent $155 high, TLI now calls this the Buy Zone and recommends a starter position here, adding more once $57 is confirmed as support. Earnings on July 30 are cited as a possible upside catalyst given the company has beaten expectations in its last three reports.

CRWV has dropped out of its bullish wedge, and TLI wants to see price reclaim $80 to get back inside it before turning constructive again. A push through the 50 WMA at $99 and then a breakout of the wedge at $115 would be needed before TLI considers the stock to have entered the Bull Case. For now the focus is purely on reclaiming that wedge support.

AEHR bounced more than 50% after a strong earnings beat (EPS of $0.11 vs an expected -$0.07 loss), but the rally stalled at the 50 WMA, which acted as resistance and sent the price back down. TLI is not calling this a buy yet and instead is watching for a deeper pullback to the confluence of the 200 WMA and 0.618 Fib around $62, which he'd view as a strong entry if it's reached.

assets.wave: Wave A down (Wave B bounce expected next, then Wave C)
assets.entry: 0.5 Fib (wait for full pullback before entry)
assets.summary: INTC has pulled back sharply after an aggressive rally with little relief along the way, which TLI reads as a Wave A decline. He expects a Wave B relief bounce next, followed by a Wave C move lower, and is explicit that INTC is not a buy at current levels. TLI wants the full pullback to play out to the 0.5 Fib before considering an entry, warning against chasing the Wave B bounce.

levels.label: Wave Count
levels.value: Wave A down, Wave B bounce expected

assets.wave: 5-wave ABC corrective pullback holding 0.78 Fib support; awaiting 200 WMA flip to support
assets.summary: Archer Aviation ($ACHR) is showing early signs of a turnaround after a difficult multi-year ABC corrective decline from its highs. TLI notes the recent +20% bounce was driven by a newly announced military eVTOL partnership with Anduril — a meaningful catalyst for the developing sector. The key level to watch is the 200-week moving average near $6; reclaiming it as support would confirm the reversal and open the path back toward prior all-time highs.

assets.wave: Clean breakout above $364 (top of Wave 1) confirmed after fakeout and 50 WMA retest
assets.summary: $AMGN has confirmed a clean breakout above the $364 resistance level (the top of Wave 1) after an initial fakeout and a successful retest of the 50-week moving average for support. TLI's focus now is on holding $364 as new support to validate the breakout, with the Wave 3 target unchanged at $470 (1.618 Fib).

assets.wave: Wave 5 (impulse, matching $V) — retested 200 WMA in June, now testing 50 WMA resistance
assets.entry: $480 (0.38 Fib) — watch for retest to add
assets.summary: TLI notes $MA now mirrors $V almost exactly in both chart structure and fundamentals, sharing a large slice of the payments market. Having retested the 200-week MA in June within the 0.38–0.5 Fib range, price has bounced back to test the 50-week MA from below. TLI would look to add on any retest of the 0.38 Fib near $480 before the broader Wave 5 trend continues higher, with a longer-term target near $805.

assets.wave: Wave 4 (ABC) — pulled back to 0.38 Fib after strong Wave 3 to $5,500 (2.618 Fib)
assets.entry: 0.38 Fib pullback zone — TLI says worth increasing exposure here
assets.summary: Gold's powerful Wave 3 rally topped out at the 2.618 Fib near $5,500 before pulling back in a clean three-wave Wave 4 correction to the 0.38 Fib, a level TLI says is worth starting to increase exposure at, mirroring the same setup flagged in Silver. TLI expects capital to keep rotating into Safe Havens during this period of broader market weakness, and is watching for the 50-week MA at $4,300 to flip back into support once Wave 5 begins. The Wave 5 target zone sits between the 0.618 Fib at $6,688 and the 1.0 Fib at $8,433.

assets.wave: 3-wave pullback from $150 to $64 — now testing 0.5-0.618 Fib Buy Zone
assets.summary: TLI flagged $RKLB as an attractive long-term hold after its three-wave pullback from $150 to $64 dropped price into the 0.5–0.618 Fib Buy Zone between $59 and $77. TLI notes $RKLB tends to trade in tandem with $ASTS, though has a stated preference for ASTS based on its moat; still, TLI believes RKLB is a great long-term hold here, and if support consolidates, Wave 3 could target the 1.618 Fib at $302.

levels.label: Correlated Ticker
levels.value: ASTS (similar setup, TLI favors ASTS on moat)
